title: ITSI License and Capacity Utilization
spl:
| inputlookup itsi_entities
| stats dc(_key) as total_entities
| appendcols [
  | rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/service
  | stats count as total_services
]
| appendcols [
  | rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/kpi_base_search
  | stats count as total_base_searches
]
| table total_entities total_services total_base_searches
description: ITSI licensing is based on entity count and KPI volume. Tracking utilization prevents license overages and supports capacity planning for service expansion.
value: ITSI licensing is based on entity count and KPI volume. Tracking utilization prevents license overages and supports capacity planning for service expansion.
implementation: Monitor entity count against license tier. Track KPI count growth over time. Project when the next license tier will be needed. Identify unused or orphaned entities for cleanup. Monitor base search count and execution time as a proxy for ITSI compute load.
visualization: Single value (entity count vs license limit), Line chart (entity growth trend), Table (entity count by type).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `itsi_entities` lookup, `itsi_summary`, `index=_internal`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Monitor entity count against license tier. Track KPI count growth over time. Project when the next license tier will be needed. Identify unused or orphaned entities for cleanup. Monitor base search count and execution time as a proxy for ITSI compute load.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup itsi_entities
| stats dc(_key) as total_entities
| appendcols [
  | rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/service
  | stats count as total_services
]
| appendcols [
  | rest /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/itoa_interface/kpi_base_search
  | stats count as total_base_searches
]
| table total_entities total_services total_base_searches
```

Understanding this SPL

**ITSI License and Capacity Utilization** ‚Äî ITSI licensing is based on entity count and KPI volume. Tracking utilization prevents license overages and supports capacity planning for service expansion.

Documented **Data sources**: `itsi_entities` lookup, `itsi_summary`, `index=_internal`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
‚Ä¢ `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
‚Ä¢ Adds columns from a subsearch with `appendcols`.
‚Ä¢ Adds columns from a subsearch with `appendcols`.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **ITSI License and Capacity Utilization**): table total_entities total_services total_base_searches


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search over calm and busy windows. Compare with the Splunk monitoring console and relevant health and introspection views, and spot-check key fields in sample events.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (entity count vs license limit), Line chart (entity growth trend), Table (entity count by type).
grandmaExplanation: We track service health, episodes, and related signals in one workspace so the team can see what broke and who owns it without juggling every console.
knownFalsePositives: Episode and score noise from content changes, seasonality, and who is in scope. We check maintenance and drill paths before we escalate.
